trl_scale.7: System prototype demonstration in operational environment
trl_scale.9: Actual system proven in operational environment

assessments.justification: SpaceX uses camera-based inspection of recovered boosters; some automated image analysis reported but not fully autonomous
Ultrasonic and thermographic NDT used operationally on COPVs and grid fins across 300+ flights
Real-time Merlin engine telemetry monitoring proven across 300+ flights with automated health checks
No publicly documented embedded sensor SHM; relies on post-flight visual/NDT inspection
Heat shield inspection partially automated with imaging; manual tile-by-tile assessment still required
Refurbishment is largely manual; some tooling automation but no fully automated refurb line
SpaceX uses flight data for vehicle tracking but no publicly documented full digital twin for maintenance planning
Flight data analysis used to predict component life; contributed to increasing reuse count to 20+
Standard camera-based post-flight inspection; lower flight rate limits data for automation
NDT used on BE-3 engine and structural components; validated across 6 reuses
BE-3 engine monitoring operational across multiple flights; suborbital profile is less demanding
No publicly documented embedded SHM system
Minimal TPS requirements for suborbital profile; inspection is manual
Low flight rate does not justify automated refurbishment investment
Limited public information on digital twin usage
Limited flight data (25 flights total) constrains predictive model development
Vehicle in early flight testing; inspection procedures under development
NDT applied to Raptor engines and tank welds in production; not yet validated for reuse inspection
Raptor telemetry monitoring active during IFT flights; system being validated
No publicly documented SHM for stainless steel tank structures
18,000 tiles require inspection; automation critical but not yet demonstrated
Rapid reuse is the goal (1-day turnaround) but no refurbishment process demonstrated yet
Vehicle digital model exists for design; maintenance twin not yet demonstrated
Insufficient flight data for predictive models; programme in early testing
Post-recovery inspection documented; small vehicle size aids manual inspection
NDT used on Rutherford engines post-recovery; saltwater exposure adds complexity
Electric turbopump telemetry provides unique monitoring data
No publicly documented SHM; carbon composite structure
Minimal TPS; heat shield inspection is manual
Low volume does not justify automation; manual process
No publicly documented maintenance digital twin
Very limited reuse flights (1 demonstrated); insufficient data
Concept phase; inspection requirements under study
NDT methods defined in concept studies; not yet validated for reuse
Prometheus engine designed with health monitoring provisions; hot-fire tested
Under study; metallic tank structures could use strain gauges
TPS requirements under definition; no inspection method validated
No refurbishment process defined; vehicle in concept phase
ESA investing in digital twin research; Themis demonstrator may include provisions
No flight data available; concept phase only
